steps.title: Fastest path: note the dimensions first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The sphere is inscribed in the cube, so it touches each face at its center. The radius equals half the edge length: $r = 78 \div 2 = 39$ inches.
